content.explanation:
**Notice Writing — सूचना लेखन:**

A Notice is a formal written announcement displayed publicly.

**FORMAT OF A NOTICE:**
---
**[NAME OF ORGANIZATION]**
**NOTICE**
**[Date]**

**[TITLE — in capital letters]**

[Body — 3-4 sentences: who, what, when, where, why]

[Name]
[Designation]

---

**Key Features of a Good Notice:**
• Heading: NOTICE (in capitals, centered)
• Date: top right or below heading
• Title: specific and clear
• Body: brief and to the point (no extra details)
• Signed by an authorized person

**Notices are used for:**
• School/college announcements
• Office circulars
• Public announcements
• Lost & found notices
• Event notices
rules: Notice = formal, short, to-the-point (not a letter!)
Always include: Who is issuing + What is the event/news + When/Where
No "I" — use organization/school name as issuer
Passive voice common: "All students are hereby informed..."
content.memoryTrick:
**NOTICE = WHAT + WHO + WHEN + WHERE**
• WHAT happened/will happen?
• WHO is affected/invited?
• WHEN is it?
• WHERE is it?
If your notice answers all 4 = Perfect Notice! ✅

examples.english: All students are informed that the school will remain closed on 25th March due to a public holiday.
A mandatory staff meeting will be held on Monday, 20th March 2025, at 10 AM in the Conference Room.
This is to inform that a brown leather wallet was found near the library. Kindly contact the school office to claim it.
The Annual Sports Day will be held on 5th April 2025. All students are requested to register by 28th March.
In view of the upcoming festival, the office will remain closed from 15th to 17th October 2025.
Students are hereby notified that the examination schedule has been revised. Refer to the updated timetable on the notice board.

mistakes.wrong: Writing a long notice (like a letter)
mistakes.why: Notices are brief — excess detail defeats the purpose

mistakes.wrong: Dear Students, This is to inform you...
mistakes.correct: NOTICE / All students are informed that...
mistakes.why: No "Dear..." salutation in notices — use impersonal formal language

mistakes.wrong: Yours faithfully at the end of notice.
mistakes.correct: Name + Designation at end of notice.
mistakes.why: Notices don't close like letters — just name and designation of issuing authority

mistakes.wrong: Using casual language: "Hey, there is an event tomorrow."
mistakes.correct: "All members are hereby informed that an event will be held on..."
mistakes.why: Notices use formal, impersonal, passive language

speakingTips: Write and read a notice aloud: 'The annual meeting will be held on...'
Compare notice with letter — feel the difference in tone and format.
Practice all 4 writing types: Application, Letter, Email, Notice — write one of each this week.

vocabulary.example: A circular was issued by the principal.
Attendance is mandatory for all staff.
The venue for the event is the main hall.

practice.hindi: सभी छात्रों को सूचित किया जाता है।
practice.hint: All students are informed = सब छात्रों को सूचना

practice.english: The examination will be held tomorrow.
alternatives: the test is scheduled for tomorrow.

practice.hindi: जो कोई भी उपस्थित नहीं होगा उसे अनुपस्थित माना जाएगा।
practice.english: Anyone who is not present will be marked absent.

practice.hindi: यह सूचना सभी को दी जाए।
practice.english: This notice should be circulated to all.
alternatives: please share this notice with everyone.
practice.hint: circulated to all = सभी को दी जाए

practice.hindi: समय: सुबह 10 बजे से दोपहर 1 बजे तक।
alternatives: from 10 o'clock to 1 o'clock in the afternoon.
practice.hint: Time = समय, AM = सुबह, PM = दोपहर/शाम
be present on time = समय पर उपस्थित रहें

practice.hindi: इस कार्यक्रम में भाग लेना अनिवार्य है।
practice.english: Participation in this event is compulsory.
alternatives: it is mandatory to attend this programme.

practice.hindi: अधिक जानकारी के लिए प्रधानाचार्य से मिलें।
practice.english: For more information, please meet the Principal.
alternatives: contact the headmaster for further details.

practice.english: It is necessary to bring the admit card.
practice.hint: admit card / hall ticket = प्रवेश पत्र

practice.english: The meeting will be chaired by the Principal.
alternatives: the principal will preside over the meeting.

practice.hindi: यह सूचना तत्काल प्रभाव से लागू होगी।
practice.english: This notice will come into effect immediately.
alternatives: this notice is effective with immediate effect.
practice.hint: with immediate effect = तत्काल प्रभाव से

practice.hindi: किसी भी प्रकार की शिकायत के लिए संपर्क करें।
practice.english: For any kind of complaint, please contact us.

mockTest.hindi: सभी छात्रों को सूचित किया जाता है।

mockTest.english: The examination will be held tomorrow.
alternatives: the test is scheduled for tomorrow.
